question: [File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: poll(PollingContext)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a subscription with a startTime set to epoch 0 and no limit, causing the retriever to re-emit all historical topic messages from the beginning of the chain, effectively presenting a reorganized view of transaction history to any downstream consumer relying on the stream ordering?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: poll(PollingContext)] Can an unprivileged user open a subscription with a startTime just before a known high-volume burst of messages, causing the poll() cursor (last.getConsensusTimestamp() + 1) to advance non-monotonically if two concurrent poll() calls race on the AtomicReference<last>, resulting in messages being delivered out of consensus-timestamp order to the subscriber?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: isComplete()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the isComplete() logic in throttled mode—where completion is determined by pageSize.get() < retrieverProperties.getMaxPageSize()—by timing their subscription to a topic whose message count is exactly equal to maxPageSize, causing the retriever to terminate early and omit the final page of messages, producing an incomplete and misleading historical record?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: isComplete()] Can an unprivileged user subscribe to a topic with a limit exactly equal to the number of existing messages, causing isComplete() to evaluate filter.getLimit() == total.get() as true prematurely if a concurrent onNext() increments total between the limit check and the page-size check, resulting in the stream terminating before all messages are delivered and creating a gap in the visible transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: retrieve(TopicMessageFilter, boolean)] Can an unprivileged user trigger the retryWhen(Retry.backoff(Long.MAX_VALUE, Duration.ofSeconds(1))) path by subscribing to a topic during a transient database error, causing the retriever to restart polling from the original filter.getStartTime() rather than from last.getConsensusTimestamp() + 1, thereby re-delivering already-seen messages and creating a duplicated/reorganized history view for the subscriber?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: poll(PollingContext)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the startTime derivation logic (last != null ? last.getConsensusTimestamp() + 1 : filter.getStartTime()) by subscribing to a topic where two messages share the same consensusTimestamp, causing the +1 offset to skip both messages with that timestamp on the next poll and producing a gap in the delivered history?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: poll(PollingContext)] Can an unprivileged user set a very large limit value such that (int)(filter.getLimit() - context.getTotal().get()) overflows to a negative integer, causing Math.min(limit, context.getMaxPageSize()) to return a negative pageSize, which is then passed as the limit to the new filter, potentially causing findByFilter() to return messages in an unexpected order or skip pages?
